title: Move Semantics 6
description: This exercise combines concepts of ownership, borrowing, and references with function parameters.
instructions: Fix the code by using the correct reference types for each function parameter.
hints: get_char takes ownership of data, so data can't be used afterwards.
get_char only needs to read the data - change it to take a reference `&String`.
string_uppercase should take ownership (String) and use it directly.
explanation: Choosing between ownership and borrowing depends on what the function needs to do. `get_char` only reads the data, so a reference (`&String`) is sufficient and avoids moving ownership. `string_uppercase` needs to own and transform the data, so taking `String` by value makes sense. This pattern of choosing the right parameter type is central to writing idiomatic Rust.
